matchups.tacticalAdvantage: Duke defeated Siena 71-65. Siena led 43-32 at halftime, but Duke used an 11-0 run in the final 8 minutes to win. Cameron Boozer recorded 22 points and 13 rebounds (AP News). Intellipick correctly identified Boozer as the overwhelming anchor.

TCU defeated Ohio State 66-64. Xavier Edmonds scored a game-winning layup with 4.3 seconds remaining. Intellipick correctly favored TCU's physical style, which heavily exploited Ohio State's 174th-ranked defensive efficiency.

St. John's defeated Northern Iowa 79-53, starting the game on a 13-0 run. Zuby Ejiofor recorded 14 points and 11 rebounds. Intellipick accurately assessed St. John's offensive rebounding advantage to correctly advise avoiding the trendy upset trap.

Kansas defeated California Baptist 68-60. Kansas initially led by 26 points before CBU cut the lead to six. Darryn Peterson scored 28 points. Intellipick accurately highlighted Peterson as the definitive talent gap.

Louisville ultimately defeated South Florida 83-79. Although the Intellipick outright upset prediction fell short, the model correctly anticipated South Florida's blistering pace causing massive disruption, as USF erased a 23-point deficit down to four points late.

Michigan State defeated North Dakota State 92-67, shooting 58.9% from the floor and scoring 44 points in the paint. Intellipick correctly anticipated Michigan State's top-tier efficiency effortlessly overwhelming the Summit League champions.

UCLA defeated UCF 75-71. Despite missing leading scorer Tyler Bilodeau, Eric Dailey Jr. stepped up with 20 points. Intellipick correctly picked the Bruins to advance despite the tight historical margins of a 7-vs-10 matchup.

UConn defeated Furman 82-71. Tarris Reed Jr. carried the Huskies with an absurd 31 points and 27 rebounds. Intellipick correctly identified UConn's overwhelming size and depth under Dan Hurley securing the win.
Florida defeated Prairie View A&M 114-55, marking the second-largest margin in tournament history. Intellipick correctly anticipated an immense blowout by the No. 4 overall KenPom team.
Iowa defeated Clemson 67-61, securing a +13 rebounding margin. Intellipick accurately identified Iowa as the high-confidence metric play capitalizing on Clemson's injuries.
Vanderbilt defeated McNeese 78-68, safely avoiding the 12-over-5 trap exactly as Intellipick projected based on Vanderbilt's severe underseeding.
Nebraska defeated Troy 76-47 for its first NCAA Tournament win ever. Intellipick correctly picked Nebraska to advance, though Troy failed to cover the value spread as projected.
VCU defeated North Carolina 82-78 in overtime, erasing a 19-point deficit. Intellipick correctly flagged UNC's late-season regression and identified this as the strongest First-Round leverage upset.
Illinois defeated Penn 105-70, breaking a program tournament scoring record. Intellipick accurately forecasted Illinois' No. 7 KenPom offense easily destroying Penn.
Texas A&M defeated Saint Mary's 63-50. The Intellipick projection favoring Saint Mary's mathematical edge was incorrect, as Texas A&M completely dictated the physical defensive tempo.
Houston defeated Idaho 78-47, holding Idaho to 28.6% shooting. Intellipick correctly projected Houston covering the massive spread backed by their No. 5 KenPom ranking.
Arizona defeated LIU 92-58, building a 27-point halftime lead. Intellipick accurately projected a massive blowout for the Wildcats based on their top-five KenPom efficiency.

Utah State defeated Villanova 86-76, driven by Mason Falslev's 22 points. Intellipick correctly targeted Utah State as the strong analytical value play against a Villanova team reliant on three-point variance.

High Point defeated Wisconsin 83-82 on a late fast-break layup. While the Intellipick prediction favored Wisconsin's Big Ten pedigree, High Point's momentum and late-game execution forced the 12-over-5 upset.
